features: Adds an explicit shell_pressure_load_path_policy argument so cached residual/JVP probes can match the current attached-components G1 frontier contract.
Adds component-block residual/JVP basis directions grouped by dominant component, component+dof, or dof.
Adds per-direction gate-limited alpha generation and a minimum relative improvement filter to avoid checkpoint promotion on numerical-floor residual changes.

followup82_dominant_component_basis.assessment: Valid attached-load-path rerun. Dominant-component basis only finds a numerical-floor 1.71e-13 N improvement, so no checkpoint is promoted.

followup83_component_dof_basis.assessment: Component+dof basis with wider support and lower ridge also only reaches numerical-floor descent.

followup84_component_dof_gate_alpha_basi.assessment: Allowing gate-limited dynamic alphas up to 1e6 does not improve beyond followup83, confirming the issue is not merely alpha clipping.

assessment: Component-block residual/JVP basis construction is now implemented and validated against the current attached-components frontier checkpoint. Followup82-84 do not open a material descent path; every positive result is numerical-floor scale and below the promotion floor. The current local row, current global Krylov, and current cached component-block JVP basis families are all saturated at the 0.03346548 N plateau.
next_solver_step: Stop spending cycles on wider variants of the same row/JVP basis family. Move to a physical residual/Jacobian consistency audit at the plateau: component force cancellation, shell pressure load-path consistency, material tangent/state update, and full-load path formulation before production HIP residency can close the actual residual gate.
claim_boundary: This is component-block residual/JVP basis evidence only. It does not claim final residual gate closure, full-load path closure, or production in-process ROCm/HIP residency.
